findings.summary: `types/shots.ts` name no longer matches what the barrel exports
evidence: The file header describes it as a shot/generation compatibility barrel, but the module path still implies shot-only contents.
findings.suggestion: Introduce an intent-revealing barrel for generation + shot types (or split into explicit generation and shot barrels), migrate imports, and keep `src/types/shots.ts` as a temporary deprecated re-export.

findings.summary: Image-to-image code uses conflicting terms (`I2I` vs `img2img`) and field styles
evidence: `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` and `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts` use `Img2Img`/`img2img*` names for the same capability.
`zImageTurboI2I.ts` also mixes `numImages` (batch input) and `num_images` (single-task input) for the same concept.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one internal TS term (`imageToImage` or `img2img`) and one TS field style (`numImages`), then map to backend literals (`z_image_turbo_i2i`, `num_images`) only at payload boundaries.

findings.summary: `asRecord` has different fallback semantics across modules
evidence: `asRecord` in `individualTravelSegment.ts` also returns `{}` for non-object inputs.
findings.suggestion: Rename by behavior (`toRecordOrEmpty` vs `toRecordOrUndefined`) or unify a single shared helper contract so nullability is predictable from the name.

findings.summary: Overloaded `context`/`ctx`/`applyContext` names obscure different data shapes
evidence: The hook accepts `context: ApplySettingsContext`, repeatedly aliases it to `ctx`, and then derives another object called `applyContext`.
These similarly named values represent different contracts (hook-side state vs service apply payload) but are passed through the same call chain.
findings.suggestion: Use distinct intent-revealing names (for example `handlerState`, `latestHandlerState`, `serviceApplyContext`) and align helper signatures with those names.

findings.summary: Feature modules named `helpers`/`utils` hide domain intent at import sites
evidence: `platformInstall/helpers.ts` contains core domain logic like `detectPlatform`, `detectBrowser`, and `getInstallMethod`.
`sourceImageChanges/helpers.ts` contains mismatch-analysis workflow (`buildMismatchMap`) rather than generic utility snippets.
Multiple feature folders expose similarly named `utils` modules, reducing import-time clarity compared with domain-specific filenames.
findings.suggestion: Rename these files to domain-specific module names (for example `platformDetection.ts`, `sourceMismatchMap.ts`, `variantPresentation.ts`, `promptSubmissionTransforms.ts`) and keep temporary re-export shims during migration.
